content.problemStatement:
Now that you've learned how to *compare* a plain-text password with an existing hash, let's tackle the other side of the coin: creating the hash in the first place!

This is a critical step in any user registration process. We never, ever store a user's password in plain text. Instead, we store a secure, one-way hash of it.

**Your Task:**

You are building a helper function that will be used in your application's registration flow. This function will take a user's chosen password and convert it into a secure `bcrypt` hash. With salt rounds value 10.
